Yesterday I baked a chocolate cake for the office birthday party.
The weather in San Francisco has been unusually foggy this week.
Reading a novel about Victorian London during my commute home.
Tried a new ramen place in the Mission; the tonkotsu broth was amazing.
My dog learned a new trick over the weekend; he can shake hands now.
Watched a documentary about deep-sea creatures last night, fascinating.
Planning a hiking trip to the Sierra Nevada mountains next month.
The new coffee shop on the corner has excellent pour-over espresso.
My grandmother's recipe for sourdough bread uses a century-old starter.
Learning to play the ukulele has been surprisingly rewarding.
The museum's new impressionist exhibit runs through September.
Bought a vintage typewriter at an estate sale last weekend.
Our chess club meets Thursday evenings at the community library.
Growing tomatoes on the balcony this summer for the first time.
The cat knocked over the flower vase again this morning.
Finished knitting a scarf for my nephew's birthday gift.
The bakery down the street makes the best croissants in town.
Started a garden journal to track what blooms each season.
The new bookstore opened with a poetry reading last Friday.
Attended a pottery class and made a lopsided but proud mug.
